flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: show this help message and exit
flags.raw: show this help message and exit

flags.name: --fix
flags.desc_zh: Attempt to fix issues automatically
flags.raw: Attempt to fix issues automatically

flags.name: --ack
flags.desc_zh: Acknowledge a security advisory by ID and exit. After ack, the advisory will no longer trigger startup banners. Run `hermes doctor` first to see active advisories and their IDs.
flags.raw: Acknowledge a security advisory by ID and exit. After ack, the advisory will no longer trigger startup banners. Run `hermes doctor` first to see active advisories and their IDs.

raw:
usage: hermes doctor [-h] [--fix] [--ack ADVISORY_ID]

Diagnose issues with Hermes Agent setup

options:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  --fix              Attempt to fix issues automatically
  --ack ADVISORY_ID  Acknowledge a security advisory by ID and exit. After
                     ack, the advisory will no longer trigger startup banners.
                     Run `hermes doctor` first to see active advisories and
                     their IDs.

summary_zh: 管理 MCP servers 并把 Hermes 作为 MCP server 运行。

flags.name: serve
flags.desc_zh: Run Hermes as an MCP server (expose conversations to other agents)
flags.raw: Run Hermes as an MCP server (expose conversations to other agents)

flags.name: add
flags.desc_zh: Add an MCP server (discovery-first install)
flags.raw: Add an MCP server (discovery-first install)

flags.name: remove
flags.desc_zh: Remove an MCP server
flags.raw: Remove an MCP server

flags.name: list
flags.desc_zh: List configured MCP servers
flags.raw: List configured MCP servers

flags.name: test
flags.desc_zh: Test MCP server connection
flags.raw: Test MCP server connection

flags.name: configure
flags.desc_zh: Toggle tool selection
flags.raw: Toggle tool selection

flags.name: login
flags.desc_zh: Force re-authentication for an OAuth-based MCP server
flags.raw: Force re-authentication for an OAuth-based MCP server

flags.name: picker
flags.desc_zh: Interactive catalog picker (also the default for `hermes mcp`)
flags.raw: Interactive catalog picker (also the default for `hermes mcp`)

flags.name: catalog
flags.desc_zh: List Nous-approved MCPs available for one-click install
flags.raw: List Nous-approved MCPs available for one-click install

flags.name: install
flags.desc_zh: Install a catalog MCP by name (e.g. `hermes mcp install n8n`)
flags.raw: Install a catalog MCP by name (e.g. `hermes mcp install n8n`)

flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: show this help message and exit
flags.raw: show this help message and exit

flags.name: --accept-hooks
flags.desc_zh: Auto-approve unseen shell hooks without a TTY prompt (equivalent to HERMES_ACCEPT_HOOKS=1 / hooks_auto_accept: true).
flags.raw: Auto-approve unseen shell hooks without a TTY prompt (equivalent to HERMES_ACCEPT_HOOKS=1 / hooks_auto_accept: true).

raw:
usage: hermes mcp [-h] [--accept-hooks]
                  {serve,add,remove,rm,list,ls,test,configure,config,login,picker,catalog,install}
                  ...

Manage MCP server connections and run Hermes as an MCP server. MCP servers
provide additional tools via the Model Context Protocol. Use 'hermes mcp add'
to connect to a new server, or 'hermes mcp serve' to expose Hermes
conversations over MCP.

positional arguments:
  {serve,add,remove,rm,list,ls,test,configure,config,login,picker,catalog,install}
    serve               Run Hermes as an MCP server (expose conversations to
                        other agents)
    add                 Add an MCP server (discovery-first install)
    remove (rm)         Remove an MCP server
    list (ls)           List configured MCP servers
    test                Test MCP server connection
    configure (config)  Toggle tool selection
    login               Force re-authentication for an OAuth-based MCP server
    picker              Interactive catalog picker (also the default for
                        `hermes mcp`)
    catalog             List Nous-approved MCPs available for one-click
                        install
    install             Install a catalog MCP by name (e.g. `hermes mcp
                        install n8n`)

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --accept-hooks        Auto-approve unseen shell hooks without a TTY prompt
                        (equivalent to HERMES_ACCEPT_HOOKS=1 /
                        hooks_auto_accept: true).

summary_zh: 管理插件安装、更新、移除和列表。

flags.name: install
flags.desc_zh: Install a plugin from a Git URL or owner/repo
flags.raw: Install a plugin from a Git URL or owner/repo

flags.name: update
flags.desc_zh: Pull latest changes for an installed plugin remove (rm, uninstall) Remove an installed plugin
flags.raw: Pull latest changes for an installed plugin remove (rm, uninstall) Remove an installed plugin

flags.name: list
flags.desc_zh: List installed plugins
flags.raw: List installed plugins

flags.name: enable
flags.desc_zh: Enable a disabled plugin
flags.raw: Enable a disabled plugin

flags.name: disable
flags.desc_zh: Disable a plugin without removing it
flags.raw: Disable a plugin without removing it

flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: show this help message and exit
flags.raw: show this help message and exit

raw:
usage: hermes plugins [-h]
                      {install,update,remove,rm,uninstall,list,ls,enable,disable}
                      ...

Install plugins from Git repositories, update, remove, or list them.

positional arguments:
  {install,update,remove,rm,uninstall,list,ls,enable,disable}
    install             Install a plugin from a Git URL or owner/repo
    update              Pull latest changes for an installed plugin
    remove (rm, uninstall)
                        Remove an installed plugin
    list (ls)           List installed plugins
    enable              Enable a disabled plugin
    disable             Disable a plugin without removing it

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

summary_zh: 对 venv、plugins 和 MCP servers 做供应链审计。

flags.name: audit
flags.desc_zh: Run a one-shot supply-chain audit
flags.raw: Run a one-shot supply-chain audit

flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: show this help message and exit
flags.raw: show this help message and exit

raw:
usage: hermes security [-h] <subcommand> ...

On-demand vulnerability scan against OSV.dev. Covers the Hermes venv
(installed PyPI dists), Python deps declared by plugins under
~/.hermes/plugins/, and pinned npx/uvx MCP servers in config.yaml. Does NOT
scan globally-installed packages or editor/browser extensions.

positional arguments:
  <subcommand>
    audit       Run a one-shot supply-chain audit

options:
  -h, --help    show this help message and exit

summary_zh: 搜索、安装、配置和管理 skills。

flags.name: browse
flags.desc_zh: Browse all available skills (paginated)
flags.raw: Browse all available skills (paginated)

flags.name: search
flags.desc_zh: Search skill registries
flags.raw: Search skill registries

flags.name: install
flags.desc_zh: Install a skill
flags.raw: Install a skill

flags.name: inspect
flags.desc_zh: Preview a skill without installing
flags.raw: Preview a skill without installing

flags.name: list
flags.desc_zh: List installed skills
flags.raw: List installed skills

flags.name: check
flags.desc_zh: Check installed hub skills for updates
flags.raw: Check installed hub skills for updates

flags.name: update
flags.desc_zh: Update installed hub skills
flags.raw: Update installed hub skills

flags.name: audit
flags.desc_zh: Re-scan installed hub skills
flags.raw: Re-scan installed hub skills

flags.name: uninstall
flags.desc_zh: Remove a hub-installed skill
flags.raw: Remove a hub-installed skill

flags.name: reset
flags.desc_zh: Reset a bundled skill — clears 'user-modified' tracking so updates work again
flags.raw: Reset a bundled skill — clears 'user-modified' tracking so updates work again

flags.name: opt-out
flags.desc_zh: Stop bundled skills from being seeded into this profile
flags.raw: Stop bundled skills from being seeded into this profile

flags.name: opt-in
flags.desc_zh: Re-enable bundled-skill seeding (undo opt-out)
flags.raw: Re-enable bundled-skill seeding (undo opt-out)

flags.name: repair-official
flags.desc_zh: Backfill or restore official optional skills from repo source
flags.raw: Backfill or restore official optional skills from repo source

flags.name: publish
flags.desc_zh: Publish a skill to a registry
flags.raw: Publish a skill to a registry

flags.name: snapshot
flags.desc_zh: Export/import skill configurations
flags.raw: Export/import skill configurations

flags.name: tap
flags.desc_zh: Manage skill sources
flags.raw: Manage skill sources

flags.name: config
flags.desc_zh: Interactive skill configuration — enable/disable individual skills
flags.raw: Interactive skill configuration — enable/disable individual skills

flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: show this help message and exit
flags.raw: show this help message and exit

raw:
usage: hermes skills [-h]
                     {browse,search,install,inspect,list,check,update,audit,uninstall,reset,opt-out,opt-in,repair-official,publish,snapshot,tap,config}
                     ...

Search, install, inspect, audit, configure, and manage skills from skills.sh,
well-known agent skill endpoints, GitHub, ClawHub, and other registries.

positional arguments:
  {browse,search,install,inspect,list,check,update,audit,uninstall,reset,opt-out,opt-in,repair-official,publish,snapshot,tap,config}
    browse              Browse all available skills (paginated)
    search              Search skill registries
    install             Install a skill
    inspect             Preview a skill without installing
    list                List installed skills
    check               Check installed hub skills for updates
    update              Update installed hub skills
    audit               Re-scan installed hub skills
    uninstall           Remove a hub-installed skill
    reset               Reset a bundled skill — clears 'user-modified'
                        tracking so updates work again
    opt-out             Stop bundled skills from being seeded into this
                        profile
    opt-in              Re-enable bundled-skill seeding (undo opt-out)
    repair-official     Backfill or restore official optional skills from repo
                        source
    publish             Publish a skill to a registry
    snapshot            Export/import skill configurations
    tap                 Manage skill sources
    config              Interactive skill configuration — enable/disable
                        individual skills

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
